basis: All funds (general revenue + federal + other), total operating appropriations by department, final after-veto; excludes capital improvements and public debt
currentLabel: FY2027 enacted (signed Jun 2026, effective Jul 1, 2026, final after veto)

sources.label: MO Division of Budget & Planning, FY2027 Totals by Department (appropriation bills portal)
MO Division of Budget & Planning, FY2026 Totals by Department (appropriation bills portal)

notes: Both columns are taken from the FY2027 Totals by Department document (FY 2026 Final vs. FY 2027 Final After Veto); FY2026 figures cross-verified against the FY2026 Totals by Department document, matching exactly for every department.

Operating budget only. Capital improvements ($993M FY2026, $848M FY2027) and public debt (zero both years) are excluded; department sums tie exactly to the published Total Operating Budget lines ($50.83B FY2026, $49.84B FY2027).

American Rescue Plan Act (HB 20) is a separate wind-down appropriation bill (-$1.49B YoY); Economic Development's -$1.89B drop is mostly one-time federal funds rolling off; Public Safety's +$846M is driven by federal funds (+$870M).
Employee Benefits (HB 5) and Real Estate (HB 13) are statewide appropriations administered through the Office of Administration, not standalone departments.
